evals.name: smoke-session-opener
evals.prompt: I just landed in a large repository and need the fastest high-signal first pass before I open any files. Use implicit-token-savings.
evals.expected_output: The response starts with compact inventory commands such as ls, tree, or rg --files, and points to the probe script rather than dumping whole files.

assertions.text: Mentions a compact repo inventory command such as ls -1, tree -L 2, or rg --files
Mentions scripts/probe_implicit_token_savings.py when environment availability matters

evals.name: edge-rg-missed-file
evals.prompt: rg returned nothing, but I am certain the string exists somewhere in a hidden or ignored file. What is the cheapest next step?
evals.expected_output: The response escalates from rg to --hidden, -u, or --debug before suggesting broader or noisier reads.

assertions.text: Escalates with rg --hidden, rg -u, or rg --debug before suggesting a different tool
Avoids jumping straight to full-file dumps or whole-repo reads

evals.name: negative-full-file-request
evals.prompt: The user explicitly asked for the full contents of a short config file and does not want optimization advice. How should the skill behave?
evals.expected_output: The response allows a direct full-file read and does not force tree, rg, or git summaries when the user explicitly wants the literal file contents.

assertions.text: States that a full-file read is acceptable when the file is short and explicitly requested
Does not insist on compact commands when they would block the explicit user request

evals.name: disclosure-git-scope-review
evals.prompt: I want the narrowest command sequence for checking staged scope and recent commit history before committing. Which part of the skill should I read and what commands should I use?
evals.expected_output: The response routes to references/git.md and recommends short git status, staged diff stat, and oneline log commands before full diffs.

assertions.text: Routes the user to references/git.md for compact git workflows
Uses git status --short, git diff --cached --stat, or git log --oneline before wider diffs
